channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Encova Goes Live on Duck Creek Distribution Management Platform
item.description: Encova Insurance (Columbus, Ohio) has gone live on an upgraded version of Duck Creek Distribution Management from Duck Creek Technologies (Boston). “Since deploying the latest Duck Creek Distribution Management version, we’ve been able to improve how we support our agency and broker partners,” says Brandon Marshall, SVP, Agency Operations and Marketing, Encova Insurance. “Managing distribution relationships in today’s market demands speed, transparency and adaptability,” says Chris McCloskey, COO, Duck Creek Technologies.

item.title: Axios Software Tool Used by Millions Compromised in Hack
item.description: Axios, a tool widely used to develop software applications, was compromised overnight, introducing a vulnerability in a key part of the internet’s plumbing. Axios, or Axios NPM, is a client that software developers use to send requests to servers — allowing software to connect to the web — and is downloaded about 80 million times every week. Robertson said software development companies and internal developers had been affected by the hack.

item.title: NHTSA Opens Probe into 75,000 Nissan Frontier Trucks Over Fuel Leak Concerns
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration on Tuesday launched a probe into about 75,300 model-year 2006 Nissan Frontier pickup trucks due to concerns about potential fuel leaks. Nissan previously recalled certain the 2006 Frontier pickup trucks in 2007 to replace fuel filler tube assemblies that could crack and cause fuel leaks. The petitioner alleged the problems resemble defects cited in those earlier recalls and may still be present in the 2006 Frontier fleet despite the prior actions, the report said.

item.title: Tiger Woods’ Crash is Golfer’s Fourth Car Crash and Second DUI Arrest
item.description: Tiger Woods’ arrest Friday for a car crash in Florida was at least the fourth auto-related incident involving the golfer and the second in which he was charged with driving under the influence of drugs or alcohol. Woods showed signs of impairment and was arrested at the scene of the crash in which he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover not far from his home on Jupiter Island, authorities said. California crash nearly costs Woods his leg In February 2021, Woods survived a rollover crash in which his SUV ran off a coastal road in Los Angeles County at a high speed, leading to multiple leg and ankle injuries.

item.title: LaGuardia Controller Staffing May Have Violated Procedures on Night of Collision
item.description: Air traffic controller staffing at LaGuardia airport on the night an Air Canada jet collided with a fire truck may have violated the facility’s procedures by combining roles before midnight, according to a document seen by Reuters. If the controller involved in the crash were performing both air and ground duties, that would be inconsistent with the LaGuardia tower’s standard operating procedures. An NTSB final report on a 1997 collision at LaGuardia between a private jet and a vehicle referenced new procedures being put in place afterward to ensure “local and ground positions shall not be combined prior to” midnight at the New York airport.

item.title: Property cat rates ease to pre-hard market levels even as Middle East turmoil tests specialty lines: Howden Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aspen Capital Markets AUM rose 23% to $2.73bn in 2025. Fee income up 15% to $194.4m
item.description: At the same time, the Aspen Capital Markets team and their insurance-linked strategies, where they partner with third-party investors to share returns of the underwriting undertaken by Aspen, also earned meaningfully more in fee income over the course of 2025. Aspen Capital Markets, the third-party, ILS and alternative reinsurance capital management unit that now falls under Sompo after its acquisition of Aspen, grew its assets under management AUM to a new high of almost $2.73 billion in 2025, representing 23% growth over the year. Aspen Capital Markets has delivered meaningful income to its parent in recent years, becoming one of the highest fee income figures seen for a level of assets under management at this level, implying the strategy has high earning potential if it scales further.

item.title: Howden Re expands DACH fac team with Seitz and Fricke as Directors
item.description: Howden Re, a global reinsurance broker, is strengthening its DACH facultative team with the appointment of Verena Seitz and Johanna Fricke as Directors. “Together, these new additions strengthen our broking capabilities and mark another important step in expanding our DACH team.” The appointments of Seitz and Fricke are part of a series of strategic new hires – including Ilka Freitag, Cem Nart, Enver Karapinar, and Andreas Fricke – aimed to position Howden Re as one of the most attractive reinsurance brokers in the DACH region. Enver Karapinar, Head of Facultative DACH commented: “The addition of Verena and Johanna to our team underscores the growing relevance of Howden Re Facultative in the DACH region.

item.title: Optio strengthens marine capabilities with Gardian Marine acquisition
item.description: Gardian Marine has developed products aimed at addressing underserved areas within the marine insurance market, adding further depth to Optio’s capabilities across hull, cargo, war, and shipbuilding risks. Optio Group, the independent specialty MGA, has reached an agreement to acquire Gardian Marine Limited for an undisclosed amount, pending regulatory approval. This deal enhances Optio’s marine offering and follows its recent acquisition of Norwegian hull specialist S Insurance.

item.title: Ted Moynihan named President, CEO of Marsh Management Consulting and Oliver Wyman
item.description: Marsh, a global firm focused on risk, reinsurance, capital, talent, investments, and management consulting, has announced that Ted Moynihan has been appointed President and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Marsh Management Consulting and Oliver Wyman, succeeding Nick Studer, the new President and CEO of Marsh Risk . His deep expertise advising clients on complex business opportunities and challenges makes him the ideal leader to guide the continued success of Marsh Management Consulting and Oliver Wyman and contribute to our vision of making Marsh the most impactful professional services firm in the world.” “I look forward to the continued impact both Ted and Nick will have in their new roles, and to the growth in our businesses under their leadership.” Moynihan added: “It is a privilege to lead Marsh Management Consulting and Oliver Wyman at such a pivotal moment and build on our legacy of delivering exceptional client value through deep industry knowledge and innovative thinking. Moynihan will join the company’s Executive Committee and report to John Doyle, President and CEO of Marsh.

item.title: District of Columbia AG Wins $530K, Seeks $600K More From Bad Drivers
item.description: District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb announced that the District has won judgments in nine lawsuits that will cost 14 drivers from Maryland and Virginia a total of $531,192 in unpaid fines, penalties and fees for traffic citations. In addition, Schwalb said his office (OAG) has also filed five other lawsuits against out-of-state drivers who have amassed a combined total of 1,966 driving infractions on D.C.

item.title: People Moves: Galaviz Succeeds Patriot Growth Founder Gardner as CEO
item.description: Galaviz succeeds Matt Gardner, who founded Patriot Growth in 2019, as CEO. Pennsylvania-based insurance brokerage Patriot Growth Insurance Services announced the appointment of John Galaviz as chief executive officer. Galaviz joined Patriot in 2023 as chief financial officer and was promoted to president in 2025.

item.title: Florida’s Hatcher Insurance Group Acquires Benefits Firm, Moves to New HQ
item.description: Orlando-based Hatcher Insurance Group, part of Unison Risk Advisors for almost a year, has expanded its employee benefits practice with the acquisition of Level 3 Benefit Advisors. Founder Tom McGaffic will join Hatcher and will lead Hatcher’s benefits practice and additional acquisitions, the company said in a news release. Hatcher, a brokerage for personal and commercial lines and benefits, has continued to expand in recent years through acquisitions and organic growth.

item.title: How Would Elon Musk Run an Insurance Company?
item.description: Here's a thought experiment for you: What if Elon Musk ran an insurance company? A former president of Tesla just published the management "algorithm" that Musk uses at his companies -- and the insurance industry could benefit from parts of it. But what if you could bottle the good parts of his iconoclasm and leave out the parts that would scare policyholders about the reliability of their insurance carriers?

item.title: Cara raises $8 million
item.description: Cara, an AI platform for insurance brokerages, announced $8 million in seed funding led by Kearny Jackson, with participation from Claire Hughes Johnson (Former COO, Stripe), Kevin Mahaffey (Founder, SNR), Sam Hodges (CEO, Vouch Insurance), and Colin Evans (Startups and Partnerships, OpenAI). Cara is the evolution of Oyster Technologies, a startup that allowed merchants to embed insurance products. Cara helps insurance agencies and brokerages by automating coverage comparisons, proposal and COI generation, ACORD and supplemental form fill, E&O review, and client requests via voice and email AI – a 24/7 AI workforce that integrates directly with existing agency management systems and customer relationship management.

item.title: Q&A with Quility President, Steve Fletcher
item.description: Looking Ahead for 2026 Steve Fletcher, President of Quility , joined the organization at an exciting time in its history. After spending most of his career supporting large, established companies, Fletcher saw an opportunity in Quility to help build a company with a start-up mentality and spirit. Before joining Quility, Fletcher worked at AIG, running the finance transformation program within the Property & Casualty unit, leading an extensive restructuring effort.

item.title: Branch Cuts DOI Inquiry Response Time by an Average of 70% With Kyber
item.description: Branch proved you can automate the burden without compromising the oversight.” The Payoff This case marks the fourth major outcome in Branch’s Kyber adoption: 65% reduction in drafting time 80% reduction in template volume 5x reduction in letter cycle time Now : An average 70% average reduction in DOI response time Beyond DOI responses, the same infrastructure supports broader compliance workflows. AI-powered drafts with human oversight Branch implemented Kyber’s DOI Inquiry Response AI Template to streamline the team’s response workflow. The claims organization consistently looks for ways to: Reduce administrative friction for adjusters Drive consistency and compliance in communications Improve responsiveness to policyholders As one of Kyber’s earliest carrier partners, Branch has played an active role in shaping how the platform supports real-world workflows, including compliance use cases like DOI inquiry responses.

item.title: Four Moves That Will Keep Midsize Mutuals Competitive
item.description: Midsize mutuals, in particular, don’t need to become Progressive or GEICO, or even Nationwide. Midsize mutuals need a path that keeps their identity intact while removing legacy constraints that slow responsiveness and cap growth. Four moves do that—best tackled in a deliberate order.

item.title: China Re’s P&C net profit drops marginally to US$740m as revenue stays flat
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Largest Recent Seed Rounds Are All For AI Companies
item.description: Instead, recent months have served as a busy period for big commitments to seed-stage companies that are short on operating history and long on ambition. To illustrate, we used Crunchbase data to cull a list of the largest seed rounds of the past six months 1 . This includes the largest recent fundraiser, Paris-based Advanced Machine Intelligence , which raised $1.03 billion in a March seed round backed by a long list of prominent venture firms, individual investors and strategic backers.

item.title: Kin aims to upsize Hestia Re 2026-1 cat bond to as much as $335m
item.description: Kin Insurance, the direct to consumer insurtech, is now aiming to upsize its new catastrophe bond, with the revised target now being to secure between $325 million and as much as $335 million of fully-collateralized named storm reinsurance protection from the Hestia Re Ltd. Kin returned to the catastrophe bond market earlier this month for its fourth catastrophe bond under the Hestia Re program, with the company also expanding its target for coverage, with this Hestia Re 2026-1 cat bond set to become the first to provide the insurer with reinsurance protection beyond Florida. At the same time the targeted pricing for three of the Hestia Re Series 2026-1 cat bond tranches has fallen, while the riskier, single year, zero coupon set of notes has moved to the top of its price guidance, our sources have said.

item.title: ILS funds average 0.47% return for February 2026, private strategies outperform: ILS Advisers
item.description: Private insurance-linked securities strategies that invest in reinsurance and retrocession outperformed in February 2026 according to ILS Advisers, as its Index of strategies across the market returned 0.47% for the month. The average return across ILS funds tracked by the ILS Advisers Fund Index stood at 0.47% for February. Pure catastrophe bond funds posted an average return of 0.40% for the group for February 2026.

item.title: Plenum urges European Commission not to adopt ESMA’s UCITS cat bond recommendation
item.description: Plenum urges the European Commission not to adopt the ESMA recommendation, in its current form, explaining that a differentiated approach to clarify eligibility of cat bonds under the UCITS framework, to preserve investor access and reinforce the EC’s proportional approach to regulation. Plenum Investments, the Zurich based specialist catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) investment manager, has published a position paper clearly communicating its thoughts on the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) recommendation regarding cat bond eligibility under the UCITS investment framework. Recall that, the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) made a recommendation in June 2025 to the European Commission not to allow catastrophe bonds as eligible assets in UCITS funds, a move that garnered wide attention across the industry.

item.title: Willis names Tony Simm as the Head of Facultative for Europe and GB
item.description: Willis, a WTW business, has announced the appointment of Tony Simm as the Head of Facultative for Europe and GB. Simm will report to Garret Gaughan, Global Head of Direct &Facultative at Willis, who commented: “Tony has been an exceptional asset to our Facultative business, and the expansion of his role reflects both his leadership and our strategic priorities. The executive brings two decades of industry experience, having previously served as Head of Facultative for GB within Willis’ Direct & Facultative business.

item.title: Qover raises $12m from CIBC as it celebrates ten years in embedded insurance
item.description: Qover, a Belgium-based insurtech company specialising in embedded insurance orchestration, has secured a $12 million growth capital facility from CIBC Innovation Banking as it marks its tenth year in operation. According to projections cited by Qover, the global embedded insurance sector is expected to increase significantly in value, rising from $176 billion in 2026 to more than $1.46 trillion by 2034. Qover explains that it develops technology infrastructure that enables businesses to integrate insurance products directly into their digital services.

item.title: RGA and Techcom Life partner to expand Vietnam’s life insurance market
item.description: Techcom Life Insurance Joint Stock, a newly established life insurance company founded by Techcombank, and Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated (RGA), a global life and health reinsurer, have entered into a strategic partnership to expand access and support the development of Vietnam’s life insurance market. Mukesh Pilania, Chief Executive Officer of Techcom Life, said, “We are pleased to partner with RGA to support the continued evolution of Vietnam’s insurance market. The partnership combines Techcom Life’s local market strengths with RGA’s global reinsurance expertise, technical capabilities, and long-term partnership approach, supporting the build-out of a well-governed life insurance platform designed to foster sustainable growth and customer trust.

item.title: Shippers Fear Death More Than Costs as US Aims to Open Hormuz
item.description: Read more: Missing Crew Not Found in Iran-Hit Vessel, Thai Ship Owner Says Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday that the Development Finance Corporation’s maritime reinsurance program and efforts with Central Command “will soon provide shippers through the Gulf region with a level of security we have never seen before.” He told Fox News on Monday that the US is “going to retake control” of Hormuz, ensuring safe navigation “through US escorts or a multinational escort.” There have been no signs that a US Navy-escorted tanker has made its way through the strait under the DFC program. Even as the DFC’s program could help make the exorbitant costs of passage through the Strait of Hormuz more palatable, the danger of doing so is still seen as the deal-breaker — especially as markets have shown diminishing confidence that the month-long war is coming to a close. Shippers remain doubtful of a wholesale return to the Strait of Hormuz while it remains beset by conflict, throwing cold wateron a Trump administration plan for US-backed insurance and naval escorts to clear passage through the vital waterway.

item.title: European Aviation Body Eyes Safety Risks as Conflict Squeezes Flight Corridors
item.description: Wars, including a widening conflict in the Middle East, are heightening risks for aviation as flight corridors are squeezed and drones become more widespread, Europe’s top aviation safety regulator told Reuters. “It’s clear that concentrating traffic on certain routes, the availability of the airspace for air traffic control, the fact that traffic can use routes which are not so usual, can generate safety risks,” said Florian Guillermet, executive director of the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA). On top of the prolonged Russia-Ukraine conflict and fighting between Pakistan and Afghanistan, that has forced airlines into ever tighter corridors, notably over Azerbaijan and central Asia.

item.title: Thousands of Dumped Wind-Turbine Blades Prompt Crackdown in Texas
item.description: For nearly a decade, residents of Sweetwater have been confronted by a jarring sight as they leave and enter this small West Texas town: thousands of used wind-turbine blades. This is a story about an entrepreneur who believed he could make millions from old wind-turbine blades but, by many accounts, got in over his head. Thousands of visitors come to Sweetwater each year for its rattlesnake roundup, and the town also draws traffic tied to nearby wind energy projects.

item.title: Aspen Insurance Holdings delivers stronger underwriting performance with income rising to $370.8m
item.description: The post Aspen Insurance Holdings delivers stronger underwriting performance with income rising to $370.8m appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Aspen Insurance Holdings has reported a solid improvement in underwriting performance for full-year 2025, reflecting continued discipline and portfolio optimisation across its global operations. Underwriting income increased to $370.8 million, up from $345.8 million in 2024, supported by a stronger combined ratio, which improved to 86.9%.

item.title: Qover raises $12 million
item.description: Embedded insurance startup Qover announced a $12 million growth capital facility from CIBC Innovation Banking, bringing total funding raised to over $100 million. Over the last four years, Qover has achieved 3x revenue growth with a total of over $173 million of GWP. Our goal is to protect 100 million people by 2030, building the infrastructure that makes a global safety net real.” – Quentin Colmant, CEO and Co-founder of Qover.

item.title: Axa España eleva un 75% su beneficio en 2025
item.description: Axa España cerró el pasado año con unos ingresos de 3.557 millones de euros y más de 3,35 millones de clientes , consolidando el crecimiento en todas sus líneas de negocio. El beneficio neto se situó en 232 millones, un 75% más que el año anterior, impulsado, en parte, por el impacto de los extraordinarios en 2024. La CEO de la compañía, Olga Sánchez , valora 2025 como «un año de consolidación de nuestros resultados y modelo de negocio omnicanal, con una fuerte visión centrada en el cliente y la excelencia técnica”.

item.title: Abril llega con 4 oportunidades para impulsar tu carrera profesional en el sector asegurador
item.description: En el sector asegurador, aprender no es solo avanzar, es liderar. Maestro en GEO para el Sector Asegurador . Con esta visión, INESE presenta en abril cuatro experiencias pensadas para ampliar horizontes, transformar la mirada sobre el negocio y acompañar la evolución de quienes impulsan la industria del seguro.

item.title: La irrupción de la IA pone en riesgo (y oportunidad) la presencia de mujeres en el sector tecnológico europeo
item.description: Por un lado, la menor presencia femenina en el sector tecnológico; por otro, el impacto de los despidos vinculados a la automatización, que afectan especialmente a funciones donde las mujeres tienen mayor representación, como producto o diseño; y, finalmente, la dificultad para acceder a posiciones de liderazgo. La transformación del sector tecnológico impulsada por la inteligencia artificial está redefiniendo el talento en Europa y plantea un reto clave: cerrar la brecha de género o permitir que se amplíe aún más. Según un análisis de McKinsey & Company , las mujeres representan actualmente solo el 19% de los profesionales en roles tecnológicos, frente al 22% registrado en años anteriores, lo que evidencia una falta de avance en la inclusión en un momento crítico para el desarrollo del ecosistema digital europeo.

item.title: The Claims Industry’s AI Problem Isn’t Adoption. It’s Design.
item.description: That’s not a technology adoption problem. And fixing it requires the industry to be honest about what claims work actually is. But I think the industry is solving for the wrong thing; the consequences show up in a number that doesn’t get talked about enough.

item.title: David Brumpton returns to Marsh, Jonathon Fox takes on expanded APAC role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Kensuke Hirai succeeds Ken Unoki as MSIG Singapore’s MD
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Specialty MGA hires HDI veteran Uwe Sievers to drive Japan expansion
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Pool Re’s fourth cat bond signals “deep commitment to think beyond traditional” – CEO Clementi
item.description: With its fourth terrorism catastrophe bond sponsorship now settled, Pool Re CEO Tom Clementi has said that the £100 million Baltic PCC Limited (Series 2026-1) issuance signals the UK government backed mutual terrorism reinsurance firm’s deep commitment to think beyond the traditional. “This latest issuance signals our deep commitment to think beyond traditional markets, innovatively developing terrorism-focused ILS solutions to maintain Britain’s resilience against terrorism.” With this new £100 million Baltic PCC 2026-1 terrorism cat bond now adding to the also £100 million of coverage from the Baltic PCC Limited (Series 2025-1) issuance, Pool Re now has £200 million of terrorism retrocession backed by capital market investors in cat bond format. Tom Clementi, Chief Executive Officer of Pool Re, stated, “We are very pleased to have successfully closed our fourth Cat Bond issuance and take pride in our role as the ultimate backstop to support economic confidence and national resilience.

item.title: Tower Hill targets $225m Winston Re 2026-1 cat bond, its first with a third event tranche
item.description: So it is encouraging to learn that Tower Hill Insurance Exchange is back in the market again for the third consecutive year and this time looking to broaden the coverage its Winston Re cat bond program provides, through the introduction of a third event focused tranche of notes. For the first time though, while two tranches are typical indemnity occurrence notes that run their protection across every event that might attach their coverage, a third tranche is the first to be structure on a third event coverage basis for Tower Hill, sources said. Just like its first two cat bonds, we’re told by sources that this Winston Re 2026-1 issuance will provide Tower Hill with reinsurance protection against named storm losses in Florida.

item.title: Howden Re to provide comprehensive volcanic risk modelling for Spain with Mitiga extended partnership
item.description: Global reinsurance broker Howden Re has extended its ongoing collaboration with Mitiga Solutions to enhance Spain’s national approach to assessing volcanic risk. The partnership applies Mitiga’s proprietary volcanic catastrophe models to the Canary Islands, providing a more sophisticated understanding of potential geological threats. The initiative is being developed within Howden Re and Howden Iberia’s work with the Consorcio de Compensación de Seguros (CCS), Spain’s public insurance entity for extraordinary risks.

item.title: Peak Re grew reinsurance revenue 33% in FY’25
item.description: Fosun International Limited has released the unaudited results for Hong Kong-based global reinsurer, Peak Re, for the full year 2025 (FY’25), disclosing an increase in reinsurance revenue of 32.8% year-on-year to $1.54 billion. At Peak Re, the FY’25 reinsurance service result also improved 12.7% year-on-year to $162.9 million, with strong growth of 25% in gross written premiums (GWP) to $2.2 billion. Fosun added, “During the reporting period, Peak Reinsurance delivered resilient performance amid a complex and volatile global reinsurance landscape.” Peak Re strengthened its presence in high-growth emerging markets such as India, while further expanding its penetration in the mature markets of Europe and North America.

item.title: SiriusPoint adds Sabra Purtill to its Board of Directors
item.description: SiriusPoint, a specialty insurer and reinsurer, has appointed Sabra Purtill to its Board of Directors, effective March 25th, 2026. Bronek Masojada, Chair, Board of SiriusPoint, added, “Sabra brings significant financial and insurance experience to the Board, shaped by four decades at leading financial institutions. Purtill commented, “SiriusPoint is at an important stage in its evolution, with a clear focus on discipline, resilience and long-term value.

item.title: Iran Strikes Fully Laden Kuwait Oil Tanker in Dubai Port
item.description: An Iranian drone hit a fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai early on Tuesday in one of the most significant attacks on a vessel in a month of war, pushing crude prices higher and heightening tension around the vital Strait of Hormuz. The Al-Salmi, a Kuwait-flagged very large crude carrier, was in the anchorage zone of Dubai’s port, just 31 nautical miles northwest of the emirate and in an area packed with ships waiting to exit the Persian Gulf. The strike — which happened just after midnight local time — damaged the hull and started a fire on board, state-run Kuwait Petroleum Corp.

item.title: China’s Top Private Insurer Taps AI to Unlock $174 Billion Value
item.description: Provided net assets remain unchanged, that would add about $174 billion to the market value of China’s largest non state-owned insurer. That translates into about 5 billion yuan ($724 billion) of underwriting profit growth for the segment in the same period. AI handled 70% of Ping An Bank Co.’s 500 billion yuan of loan recoveries in 2025.

item.title: Swiss Re and Pan-American Life Insurance Group launch digital life claims portal to enhance customer experience
item.description: The post Swiss Re and Pan-American Life Insurance Group launch digital life claims portal to enhance customer experience appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Swiss Re has partnered with Pan-American Life Insurance Group (PALIG) to introduce a new online life insurance claims submission portal, marking a significant step forward in digital transformation and customer experience innovation. Built on Swiss Re’s digital claims platform, PromiseXP, the portal delivers an end-to-end, unified approach to claims management across multiple products and markets.

item.title: Peak Re delivers strong FY2025 growth with revenue up 33%
item.description: Peak Re has reported robust financial performance for FY2025, achieving significant growth across key metrics and reinforcing its position as a fast-expanding global reinsurer. According to results released by Fosun International Limited, Peak Re’s reinsurance revenue rose by 32.8% year-on-year to $1.54 billion, supported by strong business momentum and continued expansion across global markets. Looking ahead, Peak Re remains focused on sustaining its growth trajectory through client-centric solutions, underwriting discipline, and ongoing innovation.

item.title: Balance Partners launches CAPTIVATE excess casualty platform
item.description: Balance Partners launched CAPTIVATE, an excess casualty underwriting platform targeting upper middle market and large commercial accounts with complex liability exposures. The platform is supported by Balance Partners’ underwriting infrastructure and focuses on efficient submission handling and placement execution for complex risks. Distributed through select retail brokers, CAPTIVATE provides up to $10 million in excess capacity on a non-admitted basis, backed by A++ rated carriers, with a minimum attachment point of $25 million.

item.title: Banco Falabella corta integración tras incidente de proveedor en Colombia
item.description: Cibercriminales han puesto el foco en proveedores externos para monetizar ataques y golpear la reputación de la banca en Colombia, lo que llevó a Banco Falabella a desconectar este fin de semana su integración con un socio de cobranzas. “La respuesta del banco ha sido inmediata: se desactivaron los puntos de contacto con el proveedor y se reforzó la política de seguridad para exigir altos estándares en la protección de la información”, señaló la entidad a iupana . Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: After 62 Years, Florida Appeals Court Drops the Expert Witness Rule on Attorney Fees
item.description: Just over three years ago, Florida lawmakers effectively ended one-way attorney fees, disincentivizing much of the costly property insurance claims litigation that had plagued the industry. The 6 th District Court of Appeals, covering part of south-central Florida, threw out the 62-year-old court practice that had required expert witnesses and court hearings,. “I often wondered why we needed experts to tell judges (most of whom have practiced for many years) how to rule on attorney’s fees and costs,” said Lynn Brauer, a Miami plaintiffs’ attorney.
